lesson.menu_desc: Presenting Growth Data — Third Conditional + Hedging — 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 2 — Owning the Room — Presenting Growth Data to International Stakeholders
lesson.title_tag: Professor View — Victor Malvezi Paschotto | Aula 2 — Owning the Room

listenings.text: Good morning team. This is Rachel Chen with a quick briefing ahead of Thursday's quarterly review. I have been going through the LATAM numbers and there are a few things I want to flag. First, overall revenue is up seven percent, which is solid, but it is below our twelve percent forecast. The variance is mainly driven by the Brazil pipeline, which lost traction in the second half of the quarter. Headcount was frozen in August, and that clearly impacted our conversion rate. Now, I am not saying the quarter was a failure, far from it. We outperformed the EMEA region and we reached a significant milestone with the digital platform launch. But leadership is going to want a clear explanation for the gap between forecast and actual. Victor, I will need you to walk us through the LATAM breakdown, specifically the pipeline metrics and what we are doing to address the shortfall. Please have your slides ready by Wednesday. If you need the benchmark data from last year, I have already shared it in the drive. Let us make a compelling case for Q4 investment. Talk soon.

extra_audio.text: First, open with the key headline number. Second, provide context by comparing to the benchmark. Third, explain any variance using data. Fourth, propose a solution or next steps. Fifth, close with a clear call to action for the audience.
